text.title: Tech Debt Slot Quota Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the tech-debt-slot-quota-policy methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Quota MUST be a numeric percent or absolute slot count; phrases like 'some debt time' are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Non-numeric quotas erode under feature pressure.

rule.statement: Quota MUST be signed by the tech lead and reviewed quarterly.
rule.rationale: Unowned policies fail under stakeholder pushback.

rule.statement: Sprint planner MUST refuse sprints with debt block sum < quota.
rule.rationale: Soft enforcement loses ground within 2 sprints.

rule.statement: Each debt item consuming quota MUST link to a measurable cost (perf, incident, complexity score).
Quota review MUST happen each quarter with delta documented.
rule.rationale: Without review the quota number drifts from reality.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
